title: Ligation of Sticky Ends with T4
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"16qjc","text":"Adapted T4 Protocol to reflect the one used by Northeastern_Boston for ligation of sticky end DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw the T4 DNA Ligase Buffer and resuspended at room temperature.
Set up the following reaction mixture at room temp: %s %s 10x T4 DNA Ligase Buffer 2 ul Vector DNA 0.020 pmol Insert DNA 0.060 pmol Nuclease-free Water to 20 ul T4 DNA Ligase 1 ul
Gently mix the reaction by pipetting up and down and microfuge briefly.
For sticky ends, incubate at 22 C for 10 minutes.
Heat inactivate at 65 degrees C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Chill on ice and transform 1-5 μl of the reaction into 50 μl competent cells.
creator: Joshua Timmons
